introSection: Texas Stingray is a wooden hybrid roller coaster currently operating at SeaWorld San Antonio in San Antonio, Texas, USA. It was built by Great Coasters International and designed by Skyline Attractions subsidiary Skyline Design. The attraction opened on February 22, 2020 following passholder preview days on that weekend.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Footers for the ride were poured in April 2019. Vertical construction began during the summer season.

On August 21, 2019, SeaWorld San Antonio released a teaser saying that the ride would be the tallest and fastest wooden roller coaster in Texas.

On September 12, 2019, the ride's name was officially announced as Texas Stingray. The front car was revealed at the 2019 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

On December 19, 2019, the ride had its first test run.

On February 4, 2020, it was announced that the ride would officially open on February 29, 2020. However, the ride opened early on February 22.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Texas Stingray is tall, reaches speeds of, and is  long. It includes steeply banked turns and ejector airtime moments, as well as a tunnel long.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: 2 trains with 12 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in a single row for a total of 24 riders per train. Riders are held in by individual lap-bars.

fullText:
Texas Stingray is a wooden hybrid roller coaster currently operating at SeaWorld San Antonio in San Antonio, Texas, USA. It was built by Great Coasters International and designed by Skyline Attractions subsidiary Skyline Design. The attraction opened on February 22, 2020 following passholder preview days on that weekend.

Footers for the ride were poured in April 2019. Vertical construction began during the summer season.

On August 21, 2019, SeaWorld San Antonio released a teaser saying that the ride would be the tallest and fastest wooden roller coaster in Texas.

On September 12, 2019, the ride's name was officially announced as Texas Stingray. The front car was revealed at the 2019 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

On December 19, 2019, the ride had its first test run.

On February 4, 2020, it was announced that the ride would officially open on February 29, 2020. However, the ride opened early on February 22.

Texas Stingray is tall, reaches speeds of, and is  long. It includes steeply banked turns and ejector airtime moments, as well as a tunnel long.

2 trains with 12 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in a single row for a total of 24 riders per train. Riders are held in by individual lap-bars.
